introduction:
A Death Certificate in West Bengal is issued by the Municipal Corporation, Municipality, or Gram Panchayat under the Registration of Births and Deaths Act. The e-District West Bengal 2.0 portal handles online applications.

Mandatory for insurance, property mutation, bank closure, pension, and succession proceedings. Track via edistrict.wb.gov.in.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Register within 21 days (free)
  • After 21 days: late fee + Registrar permission
  • After 1 year: District Magistrate / ADM order
  • First copy FREE
  • Track at edistrict.wb.gov.in
  • Helpline: 1800-345-5505 (Toll-Free)
documentsRequired:
Documents Required

Note: Carry originals; submit self-attested copies.
Proof of Death
  • MCCD from hospital
  • Hospital death summary
  • Cremation / Burial receipt
  • FIR (unnatural death)
ID of Deceased
  • Aadhaar
  • Voter ID
  • PAN
  • Passport
  • Ration Card
Informant Documents
  • Aadhaar of informant
  • Relationship proof
  • Address proof
Delayed Registration
  • Affidavit
  • DM / ADM order (1 year+)
  • Non-availability certificate
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via e-District WB / CRS

West Bengal uses the e-District 2.0 portal and CRS.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit Portal
    Go to edistrict.wb.gov.in or crsorgi.gov.in
  2. Register / Login
    Register with mobile number, verify OTP.
  3. Select Death Certificate
    Navigate to Certificate Services → Death.
  4. Fill Form
    Enter all details of deceased, cause, informant.
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload MCCD, ID proof. Max 200 KB
  6. Submit
    Note your AIN (Application ID Number).
  7. Verification
    Local Registrar verifies within 7–15 days.
Tip: Track status using your AIN on the e-District portal.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Register Offline at Municipal / Gram Panchayat Office

Visit the Municipal Corporation, Municipality, or Gram Panchayat.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit Office
    Hours: Mon–Sat, 10 AM – 5 PM
  2. Collect & Fill Form
    Get Form No. 2, fill accurately.
  3. Submit Documents
    Attach photocopies, carry originals.
  4. Get Receipt
    Receive Acknowledgement.
  5. Collect Certificate
    Collect within 7–15 days.
Processing Time: 7–15 working days.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Status

Visit e-District WB with your AIN.

Download Certificate

Download digitally signed certificate from the portal.

Legal Use

Insurance, property, bank, pension, and succession matters.

Grievance

Call 1800-345-5505 or visit local BDO / municipal office.
